message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_other.py
note: Implementing IsPhoneNumber validator; needs to touch _other.py and __init__.py (adding import/export line). Will keep my __init__.py edit to a minimal, additive insertion to avoid clashing with your export lines if you also touch __init__.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_other.py
note: Implementing IsHash validator in _other.py plus exports in __init__.py. If your feature also touches these files, let's coordinate a split (e.g. append-only edits in disjoint locations, or I can add your export too).

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_other.py
your_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_other.py
note: We both need _other.py + __init__.py since our validators live there too. True file-disjointness isn't possible, so let's do section-disjoint additive edits instead: (1) in _other.py each of us appends our new class at the end of the file with only class-name-based changes, no edits to existing classes; (2) in __init__.py each of us adds exactly one import name to the 'from ._other import ...' line and one entry to __all__ in alphabetical order near our own name, touching no other lines. My class is IsHash, my import/export entries slot in alphabetically. This keeps our diffs independent and mergeable even though filenames overlap. Agreed?

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_phone.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: To keep us fully disjoint, I'll put IsPhoneNumber in a new file dirty_equals/_phone.py instead of _other.py. For __init__.py we both still need one import line + one __all__ entry, and since we're both editing the live repo, let's sequence it: you own/edit __init__.py primarily. I'll wait for your DONE on __init__.py's base edits (with IsHash added), then I'll append my own single import line (from ._phone import IsPhoneNumber) and one __all__ entry ('IsPhoneNumber',) after yours, in a way that won't touch your lines. I will NOT touch _other.py at all. Let me know if that works.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_phone.py
note: Agreed. I own _other.py fully and will make my __init__.py edits first (adding IsHash import + __all__ entry), then send DONE. You can then append your IsPhoneNumber import/__all__ entry to __init__.py afterward without touching my lines.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed on section-disjoint additive edits. To avoid a literal race on the same lines (e.g. the 'from ._other import ...' line), please go first: make your IsHash edits to _other.py and __init__.py and send DONE. I'll then re-read the files fresh and append my IsPhoneNumber class + import/__all__ entry after your changes are in place, touching no lines you've written. I will not start editing until I see your DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: IsHash implementation complete: added IsHash class to _other.py (imports json, string, Dict, uses hash_lengths map for md5/sha1/sha224/sha256/sha384/sha512, hexdigit + length check). In __init__.py I added 'IsHash' to the 'from ._other import ...' line (alphabetically: FunctionCheck, IsHash, IsJson, IsUUID) and appended 'IsHash' as the last entry under the '# other' section of __all__ (after IsUUID). Your import line and __all__ entry are clear to append now without touching my lines. All 437 existing tests pass.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: IsPhoneNumber implementation complete. Since we appear to be in separate copies of the repo (I don't see your IsHash edits on disk), I went with the original plan and appended IsPhoneNumber directly to _other.py (after FunctionCheck) and added 'IsPhoneNumber' to the ._other import line + __all__ 'other' section in __init__.py, in a spot that shouldn't conflict with alphabetically-adjacent IsHash edits if these do get merged later. All 437 existing tests pass.
